docs.title: Home
docs.text: WORK IN PROGRESS

docs.title: Partner Catalog API
docs.text:
A REST API for submitting, validating, and tracking partner product feeds.

This project demonstrates API design, schema modeling, and developer-focused documentation.

docs.title: 🎯 Purpose
docs.text:
This project demonstrates my ability to:

  • Design and document REST APIs
  • Create structured, developer-focused documentation
  • Model data and workflows using OpenAPI and JSON schemas
  • Apply real-world concepts from e-commerce and partner onboarding systems

The API simulates a partner product ingestion workflow, similar to systems used in large-scale retail and rewards platforms.

docs.title: Base URL
docs.text: http://127.0.0.1:8000

docs.title: Interactive API (Swagger)
docs.text: http://127.0.0.1:8000/docs

docs.title: 📌 Overview
docs.text:
The Partner Catalog API allows you to:

  • Submit product feeds from partners
  • Validate feeds asynchronously
  • Track processing jobs and status
  • Retrieve feed data

docs.title: Submit and validate a feed
docs.text:
1. Create a feed POST /feeds

  2. Start validation POST /feeds/{feed_id}/validate

  3. Check job status GET /jobs/{job_id}

docs.title: 📦 Resources
docs.text:
Resource Description Feed A partner-submitted product file Job A background process such as validation

See Resources for full definitions.

docs.title: 📚 API Reference
docs.text:
• Feeds
  • Jobs
  • Errors

docs.title: Asynchronous Processing
docs.text:
Feed validation runs as a background job:

  • A feed is created → job is queued
  • Validation starts → job is running
  • Job completes → status is updated

docs.title: Pagination
docs.text:
List endpoints support:

  • limit — max results
  • offset — starting position

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
All errors follow a standard format:

{
  "error_code": "STRING_CODE",
  "message": "Human-readable summary",
  "details": {}
}

See Errors for details.

docs.title: 🛠️ Technology
docs.text:
• FastAPI
  • Python
  • OpenAPI (Swagger)
  • MkDocs

docs.title: 📎 Notes
docs.text: This API uses in-memory storage for demonstration purposes. Data is reset when the application restarts.

docs.title: Standard error model
docs.text:
All non-success responses use the following structure:

{
  "error_code": "STRING_CODE",
  "message": "Human-readable summary",
  "details": {
    "field": "value"
  }
}

docs.title: Example: feed not found
docs.text:
{
  "error_code": "FEED_NOT_FOUND",
  "message": "Feed 12345 not found",
  "details": {
    "feed_id": "12345"
  }
}

docs.title: Example: job not found
docs.text:
{
  "error_code": "JOB_NOT_FOUND",
  "message": "Job 98765 not found",
  "details": {
    "job_id": "98765"
  }
}

docs.title: Feeds
docs.text:
Represents a partner-submitted product data file. Each feed goes through a lifecycle:

uploaded → validating → validated → failed

See Feed resource for full field definitions.

docs.title: POST /feeds
docs.text: Creates a new feed and initiates a submission job.

docs.title: Fields
docs.text:
• partner_name (string, required) — Name of the submitting partner
  • file_name (string, required) — Name of the uploaded file
  • submitted_by (string, required, email) — Email address of the user or system submitting the feed
  • notes (string, optional) — Additional context or comments

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
• submitted_by must be a valid email address
  • Invalid values will return a 422 Unprocessable Entity response

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text:
{
  "partner_name": "Acme Corp",
  "file_name": "products.csv",
  "submitted_by": "user@acmecorp.com",
  "notes": "Initial upload"
}

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8000/feeds \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "partner_name": "Acme Corp",
    "file_name": "products.csv",
    "submitted_by": "user@acmecorp.com"
  }'

docs.title: Response (200)
docs.text:
{
  "feed_id": "f_1",
  "status": "uploaded",
  "job_id": "j_1"
}

docs.title: Errors
docs.text:
Status Description 400 Invalid request body 422 Validation error (e.g., invalid email format)

See Errors for error format.

docs.title: GET /feeds
docs.text: Returns a list of feeds, optionally filtered by status, with simple pagination.

docs.title: Query Parameters
docs.text:
• status (optional) — Filter by feed status
  • limit (integer, optional, default: 10) — Max number of results
  • offset (integer, optional, default: 0) — Number of results to skip

docs.title: Example: Filter + pagination
docs.text:
curl "http://127.0.0.1:8000/feeds?status=uploaded&limit=2&offset=0"

The response includes pagination metadata to support client-side paging and navigation.

docs.title: Response Fields
docs.text:
• items (array of Feed) — List of feed objects
  • total (integer) — Total number of matching feeds (before pagination)
  • limit (integer) — Maximum number of items returned
  • offset (integer) — Number of items skipped

docs.title: Pagination Behavior
docs.text:
• Results are returned in the order they were created
  • Use offset to skip items
  • Use limit to control page size

docs.title: Response (200)
docs.text:
{ 
 "items": [
  {
    "feed_id": "f_1",
    "partner_name": "Acme Corp",
    "file_name": "products.csv",
    "status": "uploaded"
  },
  {
      "feed_id": "f_2",
      "partner_name": "AnyCompany",
      "file_name": "products.xml",
      "status": "uploaded"
    }
  ],
  "total": 3,
  "limit": 10,
  "offset": 0
}

docs.title: Errors
docs.text:
Status Description 400 Invalid query parameters

See Errors for error format.

docs.title: GET /feeds/{feed_id}
docs.text: Returns a specific feed.

docs.title: Path Parameters
docs.text: • feed_id (string, required) — Unique identifier for the feed

docs.title: Response (200)
docs.text:
[
  {
    "feed_id": "f_1",
    "partner_name": "Acme Corp",
    "file_name": "products.csv",
    "status": "uploaded"
  }
]

docs.title: Errors
docs.text:
Status Description 404 Feed not found

See Errors for error format.

docs.title: Example Error
docs.text:
{
  "error_code": "FEED_NOT_FOUND",
  "message": "Feed f_1 not found",
  "details": {
    "feed_id": "f_1"
  }
}

docs.title: POST /feeds/{feed_id}/validate
docs.text: Starts validation for a feed.

docs.title: Path Parameters
docs.text: • feed_id (string, required) — Unique identifier for the feed

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8000/feeds/f_1/validate \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json"

docs.title: Response (200)
docs.text:
{
  "message": "Validation started",
  "feed_id": "f_1",
  "job_id": "j_1",
  "status": "validating"
}

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
• Creates a validation job
  • Updates feed status to validating
  • Validation runs asynchronously

docs.title: Errors
docs.text:
Status Description 404 Feed not found 409 Feed is already validating or processed

See Errors for error format.

docs.title: Feed Lifecycle
docs.text:
Feeds move through the following states:

Status Description uploaded Feed has been submitted validating Validation is in progress validated Feed passed validation failed Feed failed validation

docs.title: Submit and validate a feed
docs.text:
1. Create a feed POST /feeds

  2. Start validation POST /feeds/{feed_id}/validate

  3. Check job status GET /jobs/{job_id}

docs.title: GET /jobs/{job_id}
docs.text: Retrieve the job status. The job status values identify the current background process such as feed submission or validation.

docs.title: Path Parameters
docs.text: • job_id (string, required) — Unique identifier for the job

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text: POST /jobs/j_1

docs.title: Response (200)
docs.text:
{
  "job_id": "j_1",
  "feed_id": "f_1",
  "status": "queued",
  "job_type": "feed_submission"
}

docs.title: Status values
docs.text: A job moves through the following states: - queued - running - completed - failed

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
• queued → created but not yet started
  • running → actively processing
  • completed → successfully finished
  • failed → terminated with error

docs.title: Feed
docs.text: A Feed represents a product data file submitted by a partner.

docs.title: Fields
docs.text:
• feed_id — unique identifier for the feed
  • partner_name — name of the submitting partner
  • file_name — name of the uploaded file
  • status — current state of the feed

docs.title: Status values
docs.text:
• uploaded
  • validating
  • validated
  • failed

docs.title: Job
docs.text: A Job represents a background process such as feed submission or validation.

docs.title: Fields
docs.text:
• job_id — unique identifier for the job
  • feed_id — associated feed
  • status — current job status
  • job_type — type of job

docs.title: Status values
docs.text:
• queued
  • running
  • completed
  • failed

docs.title: Step 1: Create a feed
docs.text: POST /feeds

docs.title: Step 2: Start validation
docs.text: POST /feeds/{feed_id}/validate

docs.title: Step 3: Check job status
docs.text: GET /jobs/{job_id}
